---
title: "Policy and Value Functions in a HANK Model"
author: "Lorenzo Castro"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
  pdf:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

# Overview

This report documents the figures generated by the Julia project in this
repository. The model code is stored in `src/HANKPolicies.jl`, while the scripts
in `scripts/` generate the figures.

There is no separate raw-data folder in the project. The reported objects are
model-generated policy functions, value functions, and related diagnostic plots.
The main report figures are saved in `figures/`; additional diagnostic figures
are saved in `output/`.

# Project Organization

The relevant project files are organized as follows:

```text
src/HANKPolicies.jl                 Core Julia module
scripts/hank-figures.jl             Generates report figures
scripts/policy_value_plots.jl       Generates supplementary policy/value plots
figures/figure1.png                 Main report Figure 1
figures/figure1.pdf                 PDF version of Figure 1
figures/figure2.png                 Main report Figure 2
figures/figure2.pdf                 PDF version of Figure 2
output/*.png                        Supplementary generated diagnostics
```

# Main Figures

The main figures included below are loaded directly from the `figures/`
directory. The matching `.pdf` files in the same directory can be used for
publication or LaTeX workflows.

![Figure 1. Main model-generated report figure.](figures/figure1.png){#fig-figure1 width="85%"}

![Figure 2. Main model-generated report figure.](figures/figure2.png){#fig-figure2 width="85%"}

# Supplementary Diagnostics

The `output/` directory contains additional plots generated by
`scripts/policy_value_plots.jl`. These figures are not stored in `figures/`
because they are diagnostic outputs rather than the two main report figures.

## Policy Functions

![Consumption policy as a function of assets.](output/policy_consumption_vs_assets.png){#fig-cons-assets width="80%"}

![Consumption policy as a function of cash-on-hand.](output/policy_consumption_vs_cash_on_hand.png){#fig-cons-coh width="80%"}

![Labor policy as a function of assets.](output/policy_labor_vs_assets.png){#fig-labor-assets width="80%"}

![Labor policy as a function of cash-on-hand.](output/policy_labor_vs_cash_on_hand.png){#fig-labor-coh width="80%"}

![Savings policy as a function of assets.](output/policy_savings_vs_assets.png){#fig-savings-assets width="80%"}

## Value and Utility

![Value function as a function of assets.](output/value_function_vs_assets.png){#fig-value-assets width="80%"}

![Flow utility as a function of assets.](output/flow_utility_vs_assets.png){#fig-flow-assets width="80%"}

# Reproducibility

The Julia environment is defined by `Project.toml` and `Manifest.toml`.
To reproduce the outputs from the repository root:

```bash
julia --project=. -e 'using Pkg; Pkg.instantiate()'
julia --project=. scripts/hank-figures.jl
julia --project=. scripts/policy_value_plots.jl
```

The expected output locations are:

- `figures/` for the two main report figures.
- `output/` for supplementary policy, value, and utility plots.

The report can then be rendered with:

```bash
quarto render report.qmd
```
